rationale: This supervisor prioritizes forward exploration, uses commit states to avoid oscillations, and ensures safe, systematic recovery from obstacles. It always reacts to obstacles with controlled escape maneuvers and never allows immediate forward movement after detecting an obstacle in front. Commit states enforce consistent rotation until a clear path is found, then resume forward movement. Helper states manage recovery and prevent rapid switching between actions.
strategy: Always prefer forward movement when path is clear.
On obstacle_front, enter a recovery state that uses move_backward and/or full_rotate before resuming forward.
Use commit states for rotations to avoid oscillations and ensure systematic area coverage.
Rotate clockwise if obstacle_left, counterclockwise if obstacle_right, to bias exploration away from obstacles.
Never allow immediate move_forward after obstacle_front; require passing through a recovery state.
All states handle all uncontrollable events to ensure supervisor completeness.
Helper states (escape_after_obs_front, commit states) enforce safe, non-oscillatory transitions.
